# AGENTS.md — Food Delivery Platform

> **Operating contract for GitHub Copilot and OpenAI agents working in this repository.**
> Read this file in full before writing, editing, or deleting any code. Every section is binding.

---

## Project Overview

**Food Delivery Platform** is a polyglot microservices system for managing the full lifecycle of food delivery orders — from customer placement through payment, inventory reservation, driver dispatch, and real-time notifications.

The platform is composed of **5 independent services** deployed together via Docker Compose:

| Service | Language | Framework | Build Tool | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api-gateway` | Kotlin | Spring Cloud Gateway | Gradle | `api-gateway/` |
| `order-service` | Java | Spring Boot | Maven | `order-service/` |
| `inventory-service` | Python | FastAPI + SQLAlchemy | pip | `inventory-service/` |
| `driver-service` | Go | `net/http` | go mod | `driver-service/` |
| `notification-service` | TypeScript | Node.js + amqplib | npm | `notification-service/` |

**Primary language:** Java (`order-service` is the core domain service).
**Inter-service communication:** RabbitMQ events on the `order.events` exchange.
**Auth:** JWT validated at the gateway (`JwtAuthFilter`); downstream services receive the verified customer identity via the `X-Customer-Id` header.
**Infrastructure dependencies:** PostgreSQL (orders + inventory), MongoDB (drivers), Redis (distributed locks), RabbitMQ (events).

---

## Commands

Use only the commands listed below. Do not invent alternatives.

### Install / Start Everything
```bash
docker-compose up --build
```
This installs dependencies, builds all service images, and starts all 5 services plus PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and RabbitMQ.

### Run (no rebuild)
```bash
docker-compose up
```

### Test — Order Service (Java/Maven)
```bash
cd order-service && mvn -B verify --no-transfer-progress
```
This is the canonical test command. Run it from the `order-service/` directory.

### Test — Inventory Service (Python/pytest)
```bash
cd inventory-service && pytest
```

### Test — Driver Service (Go)
```bash
cd driver-service && go test ./...
```

### Test — Notification Service (TypeScript/Jest)
```bash
cd notification-service && npm test
```

### Test — API Gateway (Kotlin/Gradle)
```bash
cd api-gateway && ./gradlew test
```

### Build — Order Service only
```bash
cd order-service && mvn -B package --no-transfer-progress
```

> **There is no single top-level test command** that runs all services at once. Each service must be tested in its own directory with its own toolchain.

---

## Safe Operations

The following operations are explicitly permitted for agents working in this repository:

- **Add new REST endpoints** to existing controllers or routers in any service (e.g., add a method to `OrderController.java`, add a new route in `inventory-service/main.py`, add a handler in `driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go`).
- **Add new unit or integration tests** in any service using that service's test framework (JUnit in `order-service/src/test`, pytest in `inventory-service`, `go test` in `driver-service`, Jest in `notification-service/src`).
- **Modify business logic** in `OrderService.java`, `inventory-service/main.py`, or `driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go`.
- **Add new model fields or entities** — with the understanding that JPA/SQLAlchemy table definitions require coordinated review (see Forbidden Operations).
- **Update application configuration** in `application.yml`, `application.properties`, or `requirements.txt`.
- **Add new RabbitMQ event consumers or producers** — using the existing `order.events` exchange name (do not rename it).
- **Refactor internal service code** without changing the public API surface visible to other services or the gateway.
- **Add new notification templates** in `notification-service/src/index.ts` or supporting files.
- **Update individual service Dockerfiles** to modify build steps, base images, or runtime configuration.

---

## Forbidden Operations

The following operations are **strictly prohibited**. Do not perform them under any circumstances.

### Restricted Files — Do Not Modify or Delete

| File | Reason |
|---|---|
| `.env` | Contains live secrets — must never be touched by an agent |
| `.env.example` | Canonical secret reference — agent edits would corrupt onboarding |
| `driver-service/go.sum` | Must only be updated by `go mod tidy` — manual edits corrupt the module graph |
| `notification-service/package-lock.json` | Must only be updated by `npm install` — manual edits corrupt the dependency lock |
| `openapi.yaml` | Shared API contract across all services — changes must be coordinated across all 5 services simultaneously |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | CI pipeline — agent changes could silently disable required checks |
| `.github/workflows/agentic-ready.yml` | AgentReady workflow — must not be modified by agents |
| `.github/workflows/context-drift-detector.yml` | Drift detection workflow — must not be modified by agents |

### Forbidden Behaviours

- **Do not change JWT secret configuration** or modify any logic in `JwtAuthFilter.kt` that could bypass authentication.
- **Do not modify the Stripe payment integration** in `OrderService.java` without explicit human review — incorrect changes to PaymentIntent capture behaviour have financial consequences.
- **Do not rename or remove the `order.events` RabbitMQ exchange** or any queue name that is referenced across services. The `order-service`, `notification-service`, and any future consumers are coupled to these names.
- **Do not change service ports or environment variable names in `docker-compose.yml`** — all inter-service URLs (`ORDER_SERVICE_URL`, `INVENTORY_SERVICE_URL`, `DRIVER_SERVICE_URL`) are wired to these ports.
- **Do not modify database migration files or alter JPA entity table definitions / SQLAlchemy table definitions** without flagging for human review. The `order-service` and `inventory-service` share the same `fooddelivery` PostgreSQL database.

---

## Domain Glossary

Understand these terms before writing any business logic. They describe how this system actually works.

---

**`OrderStatus`**
An enum in `order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java` that defines every state an order can be in. The valid forward progression is:

```
PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED
```

`CANCELLED` and `REFUNDED` are terminal/side states reachable under specific conditions. **Transitions are enforced by `OrderStatus.isValidTransition`** — you cannot skip states or go backwards.

---

**`Order`**
The core JPA entity in `order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/Order.java`. Represents a single customer food order. Contains: the list of `OrderItem`s, the total monetary amount, the delivery address, a Stripe `PaymentIntent` ID (used to capture payment), and the current `OrderStatus`. Persisted in PostgreSQL.

---

**`OrderItem`**
An embeddable JPA entity inside `Order`. Represents one line in an order — which menu item was ordered, how many, and at what unit price. Does not exist independently; it is always part of an `Order`.

---

**`MenuItem`**
A SQLAlchemy model in `inventory-service/models.py`. Represents a single item on a restaurant's menu. Tracks available stock via `stock_quantity`. **A `stock_quantity` of `-1` means the item has unlimited availability** — do not treat it as out-of-stock.

---

**`Restaurant`**
A SQLAlchemy model in `inventory-service/models.py`. Represents a restaurant entity with name, address, cuisine type, rating, and a relationship to its `MenuItem`s. Identified by a UUID primary key in PostgreSQL.

---

**`Driver`**
A Go struct in `driver-service/cmd/driver/main.go` representing a delivery driver record in MongoDB. Fields include: name, phone number, vehicle type, availability status (whether the driver is currently free to accept a delivery), and current GPS location stored as a `GeoPoint`.

---

**`GeoPoint`**
A Go struct that holds a driver's current GPS coordinates: `latitude` and `longitude` as floating-point values. Embedded within a `Driver` document in MongoDB. Updated when a driver reports their location.

---

**`JwtAuthFilter`**
A Spring Cloud Gateway global filter implemented in `api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/JwtAuthFilter.kt`. It intercepts every inbound HTTP request, validates the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` JWT, extracts the customer ID claim, and injects it as the `X-Customer-Id` header before forwarding the request to the downstream service. **Downstream services must read customer identity from `X-Customer-Id`, not from the JWT.**

---

**`order.events`**
The RabbitMQ exchange name used by all services for order lifecycle events. The `order-service` publishes to this exchange when an order changes state. The `notification-service` consumes from it to trigger SMS and email delivery. **This string is a shared contract — it must not be changed in any service.**

---

**`acquire_lock`**
A function in `inventory-service/main.py` that uses Redis to acquire a distributed lock before modifying `MenuItem` stock quantities. This prevents two concurrent requests from both reading the same stock level and both completing a reservation (overselling). The lock has a **10-second TTL**. If the operation takes longer than 10 seconds, the lock expires and the safety guarantee is lost.

---

**`OrderEvent`**
A TypeScript interface in `notification-service/src/index.ts` that defines the shape of a message consumed from the `order.events` RabbitMQ exchange. Contains the order ID, customer contact details, and the new `OrderStatus`. The notification service routes on this event to decide whether to send an SMS (via Twilio) or email (via SendGrid).

---

**`isValidTransition`**
A static method on the `OrderStatus` enum in `order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java`. Given a current status and a target status, it returns `true` only if the transition is permitted by the state machine. It is implemented as a `switch` expression. **Every new status value or new transition arrow must be added here** — failure to do so will cause `OrderService` to throw when a valid new transition is attempted.

---

## Key Components

---

**`GatewayApplication`**
`api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/GatewayApplication.kt`
Entry point for the Spring Cloud Gateway. Starts the reactive gateway that receives all external HTTP traffic, applies `JwtAuthFilter`, and routes requests to the correct downstream microservice based on path prefix.

---

**`JwtAuthFilter`**
`api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/JwtAuthFilter.kt`
Global gateway filter. Validates JWT tokens on every inbound request using `JWT_SECRET`. Extracts the customer ID and adds it as `X-Customer-Id` to the forwarded request. Rejects unauthenticated requests before they reach any downstream service.

---

**`OrderServiceApplication`**
`order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/OrderServiceApplication.java`
Spring Boot entry point for the Order Service. Bootstraps the application context, JPA, RabbitMQ connections, and the Stripe SDK configuration.

---

**`OrderController`**
`order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/controller/OrderController.java`
REST controller. Exposes endpoints for: creating an order, retrieving order details, updating order status (with state machine validation), and cancelling an order. Reads the authenticated customer from the `X-Customer-Id` header.

---

**`OrderService`**
`order-service/src/main/java
